plan.summary: Address the 'button-name' accessibility issue in `src/index.html` by ensuring all 6 problematic buttons have discernible text, either visually or through `aria-label` attributes, to improve screen reader compatibility.

steps.description: **Diagnosis**: Manually inspect the `src/index.html` file and use browser developer tools (e.g., Lighthouse, Axe DevTools extension) to precisely locate the 6 `<button>` elements that currently lack discernible text. Document the exact location and current state of each problematic button.
steps.details: Open `src/index.html` in a text editor. Load the application in a browser and run an accessibility scan using Axe DevTools or Lighthouse. Identify the specific HTML button elements flagged by the 'button-name' rule. Note their surrounding context to understand their intended function.

steps.description:
**Fix Approach**: For each of the 6 identified buttons, implement one of the following solutions to provide discernible text:
1.  **Add visible text content**: If the button's design allows, add descriptive text directly within the `<button>` tags (e.g., `<button>Submit</button>`).
2.  **Add `aria-label` attribute**: If the button is purely icon-based or visual text is not desired, add an `aria-label` attribute with a concise, descriptive name (e.g., `<button aria-label="Close dialog"></button>`).
Prioritize visible text content where appropriate, as it benefits all users.
steps.details: Modify the `<button>` tags in `src/index.html`. For example, change `<button class="icon-close"></button>` to `<button class="icon-close" aria-label="Close"></button>` or `<button class="icon-close">Close</button>`. Ensure the chosen text or `aria-label` accurately reflects the button's function.

steps.description: **Local Verification**: Run the application locally after applying the fixes. Navigate to the page(s) where the buttons are located. Re-run an accessibility scan using Axe DevTools or Lighthouse to confirm that all 6 instances of the 'button-name' issue are resolved and no new accessibility issues have been introduced.
steps.details: Start the development server. Open the browser, navigate to the relevant page. Activate the Axe DevTools extension and perform a scan. Verify that the 'button-name' issue count is zero. Visually inspect the buttons to ensure no unintended layout shifts or styling regressions occurred.

steps.description: **Screen Reader Testing**: Manually test the fixed buttons using a screen reader (e.g., NVDA on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS, TalkBack on Android, or VoiceOver on iOS). Ensure that the buttons are correctly announced with their discernible names.
steps.details: With a screen reader enabled, tab through the interactive elements on the page, focusing on the buttons that were modified. Listen to how the screen reader announces each button to confirm clarity and accuracy of the accessible name.

steps.description: **Code Review**: Submit the changes to `src/index.html` for a peer code review to ensure the accessibility fix is correctly implemented, adheres to coding standards, and does not introduce any regressions.
steps.details: Create a pull request with the modified `src/index.html` file. Request review from a team member, highlighting the accessibility fix.

risks: **Visual Regression**: Adding visible text to buttons that were previously icon-only might alter the UI layout or aesthetics, requiring minor CSS adjustments.
**Incorrect `aria-label`**: Using an `aria-label` that does not accurately or concisely describe the button's function could confuse screen reader users.
**Missed Instances**: Failing to identify and fix all 6 instances of the issue, leading to incomplete resolution of the accessibility problem.
plan.rollback_strategy: Revert the changes made to `src/index.html` to the previous version using version control (e.g., `git revert <commit-hash>` or `git checkout <branch-name> -- src/index.html`).
plan.success_criteria: All 6 instances of the 'button-name' accessibility issue reported by Axe DevTools are resolved. Screen readers correctly announce the purpose of all modified buttons on the page. No visual regressions are introduced, and the UI remains consistent with the design.
plan.original_request:
Fix this accessibility issue:

**Problem**: button-name
**Description**: Ensure buttons have discernible text
**Severity**: medium
**Affected files**: index.html
**Instances**: 6
**Reference**: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/button-name?application=axeAPI
